nativeRange.habitat: Shallow coastal reef environments, typically found attached to hard substrates such as rock, rubble, and dead coral within the photic zone — the depth range reached by enough light to support algal growth.

avoidWith: herbivorous fish likely to graze heavily on macroalgae

summary: The Flat Purple Laurencia is a compact red alga (Laurencia sp.) with repeatedly branching stems and knobby tips, sold under numerous color-form names including purple, pink, rust, and yellow varieties. Kept primarily as an ornamental display specimen in reef aquariums, it is generally suited to established systems with moderate lighting and stable reef-level water parameters.
careNotes:
The Flat Purple Laurencia is the name used for a group of trade variants belonging to the genus Laurencia (family Rhodomelaceae, order Ceramiales) that are sold under distinct color-form names but share the same fundamental growth form and care requirements. The named variants include Flat Purple, Cherry Tree, Pink Succulent, Pink Knobby, Purple Intricate, Purple Knobby, Purple Asparagus, Encrusting, Rusty, Corky Brown, and Yellow Laurencia. All share the characteristic Laurencia structure — compact, repeatedly branching stems (thalli) with club-shaped or knobby tips — and differ primarily in coloration and subtle branching detail. These named color forms are available primarily from specialty online macroalgae retailers; availability outside specialist suppliers tends to be limited.

All variants are red algae kept under standard reef parameters: temperature is generally advised around 24–27°C, salinity around 1.023–1.026 SG, and pH 7.8–8.4. Moderate lighting tends to be recommended for ornamental red macroalgae in this family. Nutrient uptake is generally considered low, so these algae are kept as ornamental display specimens rather than refugium nutrient exporters — a distinction from fast-growing macroalgae such as Chaetomorpha that are used specifically to strip excess nutrients. The Color-Shifting Laurencia (Yuzurua iridescens, a species that was long sold under the name Laurencia iridescens) belongs to the same family and is kept under the same parameter targets.

All variants are generally suited to attachment on live rock, rubble, or other hard substrate in the display tank. Securing the holdfast — the root-like structure the alga uses to grip a surface — tends to improve long-term stability and growth. Good water flow has been reported to benefit overall health and help prevent detritus from settling on the branches. Propagation in the aquarium is typically accomplished by fragmentation: a healthy branch, when removed, can establish itself on a new surface. Herbivorous fish such as tangs and rabbitfish, as well as large sea urchins, may graze on Laurencia; keeping these animals separate from ornamental macroalgae displays is generally advisable.

The Encrusting Laurencia variant deviates morphologically from the typical upright branching form and may represent a different genus or growth stage; it is sold and identified as Laurencia sp. nonetheless. The Rusty and Corky Brown variants may overlap with material sold elsewhere as Laurencia obtusa or related brown-russet species, though no species-level identification has been confirmed for these forms.

IMPORTANT — ATTACHMENT METHOD: As a red alga, Laurencia sp. will MELT at the base if buried in sand. Attach specimens to rock using aquarium-safe cyanoacrylate (superglue), zip-tie, fishing line, or by wedging securely into a crevice. Sand-bed planting is appropriate only for green genera such as Caulerpa, Halimeda, Penicillus, Rhipocephalus, and Udotea, as well as some calcified species in the order Dasycladales.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Primary and sole specialty hobby source for this genus-level consolidated entry. Product page confirms Laurencia sp. identification, small branching morphology, and general compatibility notes. Detailed water parameters not stated; extrapolated from genus-level and family-level (Rhodomelaceae) knowledge cross-referenced with sibling species entry sw-algae-030.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros – Cherry Tree Laurencia
additional.notes: Secondary Mosaic listing for consolidated variant. Confirms stubby branching morphology with knobby tips; no additional parameters.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros – Purple Intricate Laurencia
additional.notes: Secondary Mosaic listing for consolidated variant. Confirms small branching morphology; no additional parameters.

additional.name: AquaMate – Color-Shifting Laurencia (sw-algae-030)
additional.notes: Sibling Laurencia entry (Yuzurua iridescens, family Rhodomelaceae, order Ceramiales) used as cross-reference for genus-level water parameters, care level, and placement conventions. Parameters from that researched entry applied here by family-level extrapolation.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
